summary: Mannerist painter named for his red hair. Known for his Volterra Deposition (men in bright robes on ladders), Moses Defending the Daughters of Jethro, and co-founding the First School of Fontainebleau with Primaticcio. Student of Andrea del Sarto.

works.name: Volterra Deposition
works.description: Deposition of Christ at the Duomo of Volterra. Men in brightly colored robes climb ladders at unusual perspectives. Oddly green-shaded Jesus. Wikipedia

depicted an oddly green-shaded Jesus in his Deposition

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Named 'il Rosso' for his red hair and 'Fiorentino' for his home city of Florence. Student of Andrea del Sarto. Co-founded First School of Fontainebleau with Francesco Primaticcio at behest of Francis I. Mannerist painter. Also painted Moses Defending the Daughters of Jethro, Dead Christ with Angels.

comprehensive_summary:
Rosso Fiorentino was an Italian Mannerist painter named for his red hair (il Rosso) and home city of Florence. His best-known work is the Volterra Deposition at the Duomo of Volterra, featuring men in brightly colored robes climbing ladders at unusual perspectives and an oddly green-shaded Jesus.

He was a student of Andrea del Sarto and co-founded the First School of Fontainebleau with Francesco Primaticcio at the behest of Francis I of France. He also painted Moses Defending the Daughters of Jethro (title figure knocking out nude men) and Dead Christ with Angels. Supposedly he and his student Barristino owned an ape that broke roof tiles by dancing on them.
